reasoning: The market resolves YES if the Nobel Peace Prize market and the bilateral ceasefire market resolve the same way. The question notes that the Nobel market has safely resolved NO. Therefore, the market will resolve YES if the ceasefire market resolves NO (i.e., there is no bilateral ceasefire in Ukraine before 2026). Given that the market was at 0.91 for YES (meaning effectively NO ceasefire) on October 16, 2025, and only roughly two months remain in 2025, the likelihood of a bilateral ceasefire being reached in that short window is very low. Adjusting slightly up from the 0.91 market base rate to account for the passing of an additional ten days without a ceasefire being in place, I estimate the probability of YES to be 0.93.
